title: Enable SCIM for Developer tenant
excerpt: How do I Enable the SCIM APIs?

posts.author: bc-charles-kramer (Charles Krmer)
posts.cookedHtml: How do I Enable the SCIM APIs?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @bc-charles-kramer and welcome to the community!

I hope you like it here

Are you referring to this?

In which case, what is it exactly what you are trying to do? What is the use case and where do you see a blocker?

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: bc-charles-kramer (Charles Krmer)
posts.cookedHtml: I am building an integration with Monday.com for our app BetterCloud. A large part of what we do is help companies provision, maintain and deprovision accounts for their teams. I can build the integration without testing, but I would never deploy it without verifying it is doing what I think it should be doing and making sure I understand all of the edge cases. Know what I mean?

posts.author: bc-charles-kramer (Charles Krmer)
posts.cookedHtml: @Matias.Monday Just in case you didn’t get a notification from my last post, see above!

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello again @bc-charles-kramer,

I am not sure I do know what you mean.

What is the use case here? What is it exactly what you are trying to achieve, which steps are you taking and where do you have a blocker?

posts.author: bc-charles-kramer (Charles Krmer)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am writing API calls to the SCIM endpoints. I am trying to achieve the following:

  • Testing SCIM API Calls

This requires that my developer account have SCIM enabled.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Ohhh I understand now!

Thank you @bc-charles-kramer!

Can you please send an email to appsupport@monday.com stating your account’s ID or slug and saying that you need to “enable the SCIM provisioning feature for your account”?

We can take a look from there

Cheers,
Matias
